external_changes.dome_site_changes: V51.1 released 2026-04-08. 2 new WINs (068, 069). Accuracy 95.2%→94.5%. New AI onboarding infrastructure (llms.txt, evidence_graph, claim_index). Sun altitude split physical<4,200km / apparent 5,733km (likely response to our sun/firmament critique). Schumann W027 acknowledged as falsified, replaced with PRED-SR-SUPPRESS. New sub-terrestrial parameter δ=6,371km (smoking-gun globe fingerprint: Earth radius).

internal_issues.description: Evidence Exhibits A and B (dome home page most prominent claims) had zero coverage in our review.
internal_issues.fix_details: EXP-036 integrated: new Section 7.12 added to sections.json part7. Covers r=0.992 (atmospheric autocorrelation), AI=1.152 (expected from Dst storms), GRACE L1A/B (JPL D-22027 pipeline), κ=1.67 circularity. Commit 9c1c324. 2075 tests pass.

internal_issues.description: r=0.992 correlation and AI=1.152 asymmetry index from dome Exhibit A were never addressed in our review.
internal_issues.fix_details: Addressed in new Section 7.12 (EXP-036). Statistical null distribution argument, autocorrelation-adjusted DOF, atmospheric loading mechanism. Commit 9c1c324.

internal_issues.description: Verdict category overload: 'Misleading' conflates 4 distinct failure modes. Taxonomy decision needed.
internal_issues.rationale_for_deferral: Verdict taxonomy restructuring requires human editorial decision on subcategory definitions. No patch can resolve this — it's a philosophical question about whether to split 'Misleading' into subcategories or reclassify non-discriminating WINs.

internal_issues.description: Kim et al. 2021 ApJ 923, 101 citation verification for El Gordo mass 2.13×10¹⁵ M☉.
internal_issues.investigation: Web search confirms: DOI 10.3847/1538-4357/ac294f IS Kim et al. 2021 ApJ 923, 101 — paper titled 'Head-to-Toe Measurement of El Gordo: Improved Analysis of the Galaxy Cluster ACT-CL J0102-4915 with New Wide-field Hubble Space Telescope Imaging Data.' Paper IS about El Gordo. Their reported subcluster masses are M_NW=9.9×10¹⁴ + M_SE=6.5×10¹⁴ ≈ 1.64×10¹⁵ M☉. The 2.13×10¹⁵ figure may be the total system mass from a different method in the same paper or from Asencio et al. 2023. DOI is correct, paper is correct, but specific mass figure source requires full text access to verify. Risk of citation error: low but non-zero.
internal_issues.rationale_for_deferral: Cannot confirm exact mass figure without full text access. DOI is verified correct. Paper is correct. Suggest low priority — paper IS about El Gordo and our argument does not hinge on the specific mass value.

expansion_integrations.note: Enhancement — ISS-627/628/629 already closed by EXP-035

recommended_actions.action: Add WIN-068 (Eclipse Magnetic Ensemble) and WIN-069 (Australia Road Scaffold) to data/wins.json
recommended_actions.details: V51.1 added 2 new WINs. Analyst recommends: WIN-068 → Std Model Explains (ionospheric conductivity during eclipses per Chapman 1933), WIN-069 → Misleading (AGD66→GDA94 datum shift is routine geodesy, 6.2% RMSE is poor by geodetic standards). These need human-authored entries.

recommended_actions.action: Update accuracy critique from 95.2% to 94.5% in sections.json
recommended_actions.details: V51.1 changed dome accuracy from 95.2% to 94.5%. Section 6.6 heading, the HTML div code, and the formula need updating. This should wait until WIN-068/069 are added so TOTAL_WINS is correct. Key nuance: the change actually strengthens our argument — the number is not frozen, it fluctuates with each model update, yet it's still manually entered HTML.

recommended_actions.action: Document V51.1 AI steering infrastructure in Part 2b
recommended_actions.details: V51.1 added llms.txt, evidence_graph, claim_index — public-facing AI narrative control layer on top of existing inject_ai_layer.py. This is significant escalation: covert (code-level) → overt (standards-compliant) AI onboarding. Document in sections.json part2b.

recommended_actions.action: Update sun/firmament collision argument for V51.1 physical/apparent split
recommended_actions.details: Dome now claims physical sun <4,200 km vs apparent 5,733 km via refraction. Our current text should note this as an attempted fix that makes things worse: (1) inequality is unfalsifiable, (2) refraction needed is ~1,533km vertical — physically impossible, (3) makes ALL triangulation measurements unreliable. Suggest analyst EXP for targeted update.

Human editorial decision required. Option A: add subcategory clarification to 6.4 prose (already done in EXP-008). Option B: split 'Misleading' into 'Data Misrepresentation' + 'Non-Discriminating'. Option C: move non-discriminating WINs to 'Std Model Explains'. Each has trade-offs.

recommended_actions.action: Check Polaris WIN-065 for strengthening opportunity
recommended_actions.details: Analyst WIN-065 fingerprint: TRIPLE self-contradiction (constant H=4750 vs H(r) formula, PRED-NEW-001 vs coordinate table, r=4750/tan(globe_latitude) is a globe-latitude function). Current WIN-065 text may not reflect these findings. Consider analyst EXP for WIN-065 update.
